title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size of the list is even, there is no middle value, and the median is the mean of the two middle values. Implement the MedianFinder class: MedianFinder() initializes the object; void addNum(int num) adds the integer num from the data stream to the data structure; double findMedian() returns the median of all elements so far.
constraints: There will be at least one element in the data structure before calling findMedian
At most 5 * 10^4 calls will be made to addNum and findMedian
thinking_questions: The median is the middle value. If you split the stream into two halves, what's the relationship between the max of the lower half and min of the upper half?
What data structure gives you the max of the lower half in O(1)?
How do you keep the two halves balanced in size?

hints.L1: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half. Median = top of one or average of both tops.
hints.L2: Max-heap (lower half) and min-heap (upper half). Keep sizes equal or lower has one more. On addNum: rebalance. getMedian: peek tops.
hints.L3:
# INSIGHT: two heaps split at median — O(log n) add, O(1) get
import heapq
self.small = []  # max-heap (store negated)
self.large = []  # min-heap

def addNum(num):
    heapq.heappush(small, -num)  # push to lower half
    # ensure max(small) <= min(large)
    if small and large and -small[0] > large[0]:
        heapq.heappush(large, -heapq.heappop(small))
    # balance sizes: small can have at most 1 extra
    if len(small) > len(large)+1:
        heapq.heappush(large, -heapq.heappop(small))
    if len(large) > len(small):
        heapq.heappush(small, -heapq.heappop(large))

def findMedian():
    if len(small) > len(large): return -small[0]
    return (-small[0] + large[0]) / 2

follow_up_questions: Why use a max-heap for the lower half and min-heap for the upper half?
What's the rebalancing logic — what conditions trigger a move between heaps?
If the stream consisted of only integers from [0..100], how would you optimize with a frequency array?
solution_notes: Two heaps: max-heap lower, min-heap upper. Rebalance on each add. O(log n) add, O(1) median.
